title: storage-v3 #1: replace try_commit_dance with git-only write path
body:
# Goal

Replace `Storage::try_commit_dance` (the 4-CLI jj write dance,
`crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:2836â€“2860`) with a git-only write
path that lands every mutation as a commit on a per-issue ref
under `refs/jjf/issues/<id>` (or `refs/jjf/memories/<key>` for
memories).

End state: every `Storage` mutating method (create_issue,
update, add_comment, close, open, claim, unclaim, block, unblock,
add_label, rm_label, add_dep_edge, rm_dep_edge, set_memory,
unset_memory, etc.) lands its commit via `git update-ref` CAS,
not via `jj new` / `jj bookmark set` / `jj new root()`. Git HEAD
never moves during a write. The jj working copy is never
touched.

# Why now

Foundation of the v3 storage redesign. See
`docs/storage-out-of-tree.md` for the full design verdict and
`bd98097` for the implementation epic. This is ticket 1 of 8;
2 (read path) and 4 (v2â†’v3 migrator) depend on this landing
first so they have a v3 write path to call.

# Approach

Per the design doc's "Write path" section:

1. **Read current state.** `git cat-file blob refs/jjf/issues/<id>:issue.json`
   (or treat as empty for `create`).
2. **Apply the op in memory.** Reuse the existing in-memory
   logic that produces the next `Record` snapshot â€” that code
   stays.
3. **Build a tree.** `git hash-object -w --stdin` for each
   blob (`issue.json`, `comments.jsonl` if present), then
   `git mktree` to assemble the tree.
4. **Build a commit.** `git commit-tree <tree> -p <prev-tip> -m <msg>`
   for an update, no `-p` for a `create`. `<msg>` is the
   summary line + trailer block produced by `Op::to_trailer_block`
   (unchanged).
5. **Atomically update the ref.** `git update-ref
   refs/jjf/issues/<id> <new-commit> <old-commit>` â€” third arg
   is the CAS sentinel. On CAS failure, the existing
   concurrent-write retry policy applies (`434a2f87`): one
   retry with re-read, jittered sleep.

**Preserve as-is:**
- Trailer serialization (`op.rs::Op::to_trailer_block`).
- Boundary validation (`validate_title`, `validate_no_newlines`,
  self-dep check, phantom-target check).
- Multi-op-per-commit support (the new write path accepts a
  `Vec<Op>` and emits N trailer stanzas in one commit message,
  same shape as today's multi-trailer commits).

**Bypass entirely:**
- `jj new bookmarks(issues) -m ...` (step 1 of the dance).
- `jj bookmark set issues -r @ --allow-backwards` (step 3).
- `jj new root()` (step 4, the HEAD-drift trigger).

# Scope

In scope:
- New `crates/jjf-storage/src/git.rs` (or similar) module
  housing the git-CLI write helpers (`hash_object`, `mktree`,
  `commit_tree`, `update_ref`).
- Refactor `try_commit_dance` callers to route through the new
  write path.
- Per-issue-ref CAS retry loop, sharing the existing
  `is_concurrent_write` policy where possible.
- New write path covers issues AND memories (the design's
  `refs/jjf/memories/<key>` namespace).

Out of scope (handled by later tickets):
- Read path repointing (ticket 2).
- `Storage::init` rewrite (ticket 3).
- v2 â†’ v3 auto-migrator (ticket 4).
- Push/pull refspec (ticket 5).
- Snapshot cache reuse (ticket 6).
- Test sweep (ticket 7).
- Self-host guard removal (ticket 8).

**Critical:** existing v2 tests and read paths must KEEP WORKING
through this ticket. The v2 â†’ v3 cutover happens in ticket 4. So
the write path needs to be added ALONGSIDE the v2 dance, gated
by some flag (env var, feature, or runtime detection), with the
v2 dance still operating on the `issues` bookmark for existing
repos. Easiest gate: detect at `Storage::open` whether v3 refs
exist; if yes, route writes to v3; if no, keep using the v2
dance. Same dispatch pattern as the v1â†’v2 detection.

# Acceptance

- [ ] New `git.rs` (or equivalent) module landed with the four
      helper fns (`hash_object`, `mktree`, `commit_tree`,
      `update_ref`).
- [ ] Every `Storage` mutating method routes through the new
      write path when v3 refs are detected.
- [ ] The 4-CLI dance still operates when v2 refs are detected
      (v2 tests pass unchanged).
- [ ] Concurrent-write retry policy works on the v3 path
      (existing `concurrent_write_retry_preserves_both_writes`
      style test passes on v3).
- [ ] No `jj` subprocess invoked on a v3 write path. Verify by
      grepping the write path code; no `Command::new("jj")`.
- [ ] A new integration test creates an issue in a v3-shape
      repo, mutates it, and verifies git HEAD does not move
      and the jj working copy is unchanged across mutations.
- [ ] `cargo nextest run --workspace` green.

# Pointers

- Design doc: `docs/storage-out-of-tree.md`.
- Code to replace: `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs:2836â€“2860`
  (`try_commit_dance`).
- Code to preserve: `crates/jjf-storage/src/op.rs`
  (`Op::to_trailer_block`), `crates/jjf-storage/src/trailer.rs`
  (the parser), `crates/jjf-storage/src/history.rs`
  (history walker â€” still used by reads in v2 mode).
- Concurrent-write retry policy: `434a2f87` (commit landed it),
  `crates/jjf-storage/src/lib.rs` (the retry loop in `mutate`
  / `add_comment` / `create_issue`).
- Prior art: git-bug's write path; 34 archived `refs/bugs/*`
  refs in this repo (`git for-each-ref refs/bugs/` shows the
  shape).

# Out-of-scope reminder for the subagent

Do NOT change the trailer format, the op vocabulary, the
`Record` / `Comment` / `Memory` JSON schemas, or the CLI
surface. This is a storage-layer-only refactor.
